claim: OpenCode is a full-screen TUI (Terminal User Interface) unlike Cloud Code's CLI approach—full screen rendering enables more complex UI features like file explorers, diffs, and modals
actionable: When building terminal AI interfaces, consider full-screen TUI for richer interactions; minimize TUI for simpler workflows where traditional CLI output suffices.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: OpenCode implements client-server architecture with TypeScript/Bun backend compiled to native executables (no Node.js dependency) and configurable frontends—enables dynamic provider downloading and custom JavaScript tools
actionable: Use Bun for building AI tools in TypeScript with zero dependencies; structure as server+client to decouple logic from UI implementation.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: Code review, not code generation, is the core developer workflow with LLMs—design tools for reviewing code diffs, reverting changes, understanding why changes were made rather than focusing on editor functionality
actionable: Build code AI tools around diff viewing and validation rather than attempting to replace editors; position the AI tool as a review and approval layer.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: Building a full editor in an AI tool is an infinite rabbit hole of complexity (syntax highlighting, formatting, linting integration)—better to focus on code review features and let users keep their preferred editor
actionable: Don't attempt to build editor functionality into AI coding tools; instead integrate with existing editors or focus on non-editing features like review and diffs.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: Snapshot/shareable session feature in OpenCode enables PR context sharing—reviewers can see the full conversation history, tool calls, and diffs to understand code generation rationale
actionable: Implement session snapshots with full conversation history, tool invocations, and file diffs for collaborative code review and learning.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: Modes system in OpenCode allows switching between plan mode (exploration) and implement mode (execution) with different models, tools, and prompts per mode—tab to cycle between modes
actionable: Design multi-mode agentic tools where modes are combinations of system prompts, model selections, and tool sets; allow users to switch modes for different phases of work.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: Trying to optimize LLM behavior with fancy tricks (specialized edit models, complex fallback strategies) often wastes time—frontier models eventually make those tricks obsolete; focus on product experience instead
actionable: Avoid building complex optimizations on top of LLMs; instead focus on product features that will remain valuable even as models improve.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: File edit fallback strategies from Gemini's implementation were problematic and created regression loops—copying strategies blindly from other codebases without validation is risky
actionable: When implementing file editing logic, thoroughly test fallback strategies on your codebase; don't assume strategies from other projects will work identically.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: Sonnet 4 is currently the only frontier model that works well for code AI tools; Cloud Code's momentum and brand recognition make it dominant unless another lab releases a competitive model
actionable: When choosing models for code AI, benchmark on your actual codebase; don't default to expensive frontier models if open-source performs adequately.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: Most LLM evals (mazes, academic benchmarks) don't resemble real developer workflows—use custom evals that reflect actual work (implement features, create data models, migrations, APIs) to measure product quality
actionable: Build internal benchmarks that mirror your actual use cases; don't rely on standard LLM benchmarks to validate product quality.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: Context window compaction is an 80% problem—most users start new sessions frequently and don't hit long-running scenarios; focus on product experience for the common case rather than edge-case optimization
actionable: Prioritize product experience for typical workflows; defer optimization of edge cases like extreme context window exhaustion until they impact most users.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI

claim: For monetizing open-source AI tools, serve enterprise customers who need team management, internal API integration, and usage metrics—avoid monetizing the open-source project itself
actionable: Keep open-source tools free; monetize through commercial offerings for enterprise features like team management, API integration, and compliance.
source_title: OpenCode: Claude Code but Open Source, with Any Model, and frontier TUI
